name: Splash Music
tagline: Generative AI music creation and beatmaker app from Brisbane studio Popgun
description: Splash Music — formerly known as Popgun — was a Brisbane, Australia studio that built one of the first viral generative AI music apps, letting users create songs from text prompts with AI-generated vocals and instrumental beats. Founded in 2017, Popgun raised roughly $20 million in a Series A co-led by Amazon's Alexa Fund and Bitkraft Ventures in 2021. The consumer music apps Splash Music & Beatmaker and Splash Pro were both discontinued in 2024, with the company announcing it would return entirely to its roots in video game development.
story:
Popgun started in 2017 as a research lab betting that AI could compose music. By 2020 the team had pivoted into consumer apps, launching Splash — a free iOS and Android beatmaker that let users build tracks by tapping loops in time with a metronome — and growing into a Roblox phenomenon where millions of monthly active users built and remixed music inside the game. The 2021 Series A, co-led by Amazon's Alexa Fund and Bitkraft Ventures, took total funding to around $20 million and was sized for a future in which generative AI would expand from beats into full songs with vocals.

That future arrived faster than expected, and not in Splash's favor. Splash Pro launched as a web-based generative AI music tool with AI vocals, riding the wave of consumer interest in tools like ChatGPT and Midjourney. But the ground shifted underneath: Suno and Udio raised hundreds of millions of dollars, shipped vastly better text-to-song models, and absorbed the same consumer demand. Splash Pro's vocal quality and prompt control couldn't keep up, and the consumer mobile beatmaker was a side bet competing with the company's own much more profitable Roblox game.

In early 2024 the company made the call to abandon the music apps and refocus on gaming. Splash Music & Beatmaker shut down on January 16, 2024. Splash Pro was discontinued on April 22, 2024 after just eight months in business. The company's blog post framed it as a return to roots — and the Roblox game and a new Steam title were genuinely thriving — but it was also a clean admission that an AI music wrapper, even one with $20 million in venture funding and Amazon as an investor, could not compete against well-capitalized model labs that had passed it on the technology curve.

sources.title: Splash Music AI Generator Shuts Down, Pivots Back Into Video Games
Splash raises $20m to develop its music game and find stars
